metadata.dataset_description: Hourly time series (multiple measured features) from 2016-07-01 to 2018-06-26. Contains 7 numeric sensor/measurement columns and one timestamp column. Likely target column named 'OT' (continuous).
known_data_quality_issues: Negative values present in columns that are likely non-negative (e.g., HUFL min -22.706, MUFL min -25.088, OT min -4.08, LUFL min -1.188). Verify whether these are valid or measurement/encoding errors.
Outliers flagged (IQR rule) in HUFL, MUFL, LUFL, LULL and OT (several percent of rows).
No missing values detected but there are irregular extreme values that may need correction or removal.
All timestamps are regular hourly, but columns may require unit checks and shifting before log-transform due to negative minima.

preprocessing_steps.reason: IQR-rule outliers detected in multiple numeric columns (some extreme negative/positive measurements). Remove or cap them early to avoid distortion of detrending/scaling.
A persistent (slight) linear trend is present (linear regression on OT shows a non-zero slope and R^2 ~0.39). Detrending helps models that assume stationarity and reduces leakage from trend.
OT and LUFL show right-skew (OT skew ~0.97, LUFL skew >1). Apply log after shifting to stabilize variance and reduce skewness before scaling and differencing.

Strong daily (lag 24) and weekly (lag 168) autocorrelations detected (e.g., ACF(24) ~0.94, ACF(168) ~0.86). Seasonal differencing (lag 24) and first differencing help achieve stationarity for many forecasting methods.
Features are on different scales; z-score normalization ensures comparable scales for gradient-based and distance-based models.

Hourly measurements show high autocorrelation and occasional high-frequency noise. A small rolling window (3) smooths noise while preserving hourly dynamics; optionally apply only for feature engineering, not label smoothing.

metadata.licenses_or_usage_rights: Dataset was provided by the user. Confirm original source and license before redistribution or commercial use.
metadata.security_or_privacy_notes: No explicit PII detected in this dataset; it contains timestamps and measurement values. If these measurements can be linked to individuals or sensitive systems, apply appropriate privacy controls. Keep raw timestamps when sharing only if permitted.

model_reasoning.LightGBM (with lag features & exogenous: Tree-based gradient boosting handles heterogeneous features, is robust to outliers after capping, can use engineered lag/rolling features, and works well on moderate-sized datasets (~17k rows).
model_reasoning.Prophet (or SARIMAX with exogenous regre: Designed for time series with trend and multiple seasonality components (daily/weekly). SARIMAX can incorporate exogenous variables; Prophet automatically models trend and seasonality.
model_reasoning.LSTM (sequence-to-one, multivariate): RNN-based model that can learn temporal dependencies from multivariate hourly sequences; appropriate if you want to capture complex nonlinear interactions and have sufficient training data.
model_reasoning.N-BEATS (deep learning, interpretable tr: Modern deep learning forecasting model that separates trend/seasonality blocks; works well for mid-sized time series and often outperforms classical methods on complex patterns.
